role_summary: Pioneering Black journalist; Baltimore Afro-American reporter who covered the September 1955 Emmett Till trial and the 1961 Freedom Rides. Born 1927, died 2024.
ai_reading: Moses Newson shares the embedding signature of the joint oral history at entry 9 (catalog slug 'simeon-booker-and-moses-newson'); see that catalog page for the substrate-derived neighbor cluster and axis observations the embedding produces for the combined transcript. Individual cosines for each named subject of a joint interview are not separable from the precomputed substrate, since the LoC transcript was recorded as a single audio file with multiple voices interleaved.
biographical_paragraph: Moses Newson is one of the named subjects of the joint Civil Rights History Project oral history at entry 9 (American Folklife Center accession AFC 2010/039) [src: 1]. This catalog page exists as a per-person index for Moses Newson; the substrate signature lives on the parent joint-interview page (see the AI's reading above) since the LoC transcript treats the joint conversation as a single recording. Independent biographical sourcing for Moses Newson is pending.

sources.title: Joint oral history at entry 9
sources.publisher: Civil Rights History Project, Library of Congress (AFC 2010/039)
